item.title: Let’s Talk About Contrails!
item.description:
https://www.rollingstone.com/politics/politics-features/chemtrails-banning-laws-1235488069/ I’m sure everyone has heard of the conspiracy theory known as “chemtrails”. And more recently, states like Tennessee and Florida have passed bills trying to band this so called weather modification. But what’s the real science behind contrails from planes, and are they really bad for the environment? Come with me on a cloud filled journey.
HOW CLOUDS FORM So first we need to understand how a cloud forms, since contrails are clouds. Basically all clouds need water vapor, but it needs some help. The help comes from small particulates called Condensation Nuclei: dust, dirt, pollutants that help water vapor to condense and form into droplets. These droplets then combine and form into large clouds. Depending on the height of the cloud, these droplets can freeze and turn into ice crystals, which is why some clouds look hazy and spread out (cirrus) vs ones that are puffy (cumulus)
CONTRAILS ARE CIRRUS CLOUDS Alright, so now we know how clouds form, let’s talk contrails. First and foremost, these contrails only happen when a plane is near cruising altitude. Typically 30-40k feet up. Also interesting is that Cirrus clouds only form above 20k feet. So it’s pretty easy to see that contrails are just man-made cirrus clouds. Cirrus clouds need specific conditions to form, so it’s why you see some contrails last and others dissipate. Basically, exhaust from the jet engine releases heat around it, cooling the air and turning water vapor to condensed cloud droplets. Remember: exhaust is a pollutant, just like the exhaust from our cars. They can also produce particulates into the atmosphere, which in this case help form said contrail clouds.
CONTRAILS, NOT CHEMTRAILS Contrails are not purposely sprayed chemicals into the atmosphere. They are not deliberately created to modify the weather or produce harmful effects to the surface dwellers. There is no large organization doing this behind the FAA’s back. Contrails are a natural byproduct of engine exhaust being exposed to very cold and moist environment. They happen all around the world too, so this is not just a symptom of being in the States.
ARE CONTRAILS DANGEROUS? https://www.earthdata.nasa.gov/news/feature-articles/trail-contrails Short Answer: No. Contrails have been around since the age of jet engines. With increased air travel in the past 40-50 years, it’s no wonder the older population is seeing more and more (prompting silly weather modification bills). But: there are some concerns. For one, jet engine emissions are producing CO2 into the atmosphere, which is a greenhouse gas. Secondly, resulting contrails that don’t dissipate can cause almost a blanket effect, causing radiation to reflect and lead to cooling/heating. So while these contrails are not spewing chemicals, they can have global, longer term effects.
Cat Cloud Tax Thank you coming along. The Great Cat Cloud is pleased. So as you can see, contrails are manmade cirrus clouds. They are not chemicals or purposely done. They do pose issues to our environment (greenhouse gasses and albedo reflecting). And unfortunately, unless we modify the weather at 30-40k feet, or completely remove combustion engines from planes, contrails are never going away as long as planes fly!
item.dc:creator: WeatherWiz

item.title: A way out
item.description: There you go. Here's the solution. A lot of people with other civilians and they realize that military service members in America are more property than not. This would be a way to get out with either an other than honorable or dishonorable. This is the way.

item.title: Fuck this nail in particular!
item.description:
Source: https://www.reddit.com/r/NatureIsFuckingLit/s/p7GAw7e8k2 . I was going through my saved links on my Reddit account, and I found this gem. When I first watched it, I thought this: I’m not sure what this nail did to this little mason bee, but it clearly had to fucking go! I did some research on mason bees, and found out that they’re solitary bees! Apparently a single female will burrow in a suitable hole and raise her own young.
Image Source: https://www.nhsn.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/solitary-bee-nest.png . This is what the inside of the mason bee looks like. Another fascinating thing about them is that they don’t make honey. Instead, they eat the pollen and nectar directly. At least, that’s how another source worded it: “Mason bees do not make honey. They eat pollen and nectar throughout their lives as they forage. There is no need to create stores of food as the adult bees die before the weather gets cold and the species overwinter as pupae. The pupae will emerge when the weather warms in the spring.” . I wonder if this solitary lifestyle makes a mason bee more nurturing towards their youth. Insects obviously don’t have the same emotional spectrum as a mammal, but there have been some studies that suggest that insects have more emotional capacity than we give them credit for. I wonder if these mother bees care more for their youth than bees in a hive that are easily replaced if they die… if that makes any sense whatsoever.
It seems like the life of a mason bee is a perpetual refusal to get out of bed. Completely understandable.
And she was successful in getting rid of it.
The Crown Bees Website: https://crownbees.com/pages/masonbees United States Department of Agriculture’s Overview on Mason Bees: https://www.fs.usda.gov/wildflowers/pollinators/pollinator-of-the-month/mason_bees.shtml Natural History Society of Northumbria: https://www.nhsn.org.uk/a-short-guide-to-solitary-bees/ Oregon State University: https://blogs.oregonstate.edu/gardenecologylab/2021/06/28/spring-with-the-mason-bees/ The Article I Quoted in This Post: https://www.keepingbackyardbees.com/6-amazing-facts-about-mason-bees-zbwz1712zsau/

item.title: GovBrief Today #415
item.description: Judge Douglas Micko rebuked federal prosecutors Friday for charging a Minnesota woman with conspiring to violate religious freedom at a church protest she never attended, writing, "the government had an intense appetite to initiate" the case. Fifty-year-old Heather Danae Lewis picked up auction items from a parking lot where church protesters later gathered. Prosecutors traced her cellphone to the location, matched her driver's license photo to a woman in the video, and charged her with two federal crimes. Federal agents pulled her over and interrogated her for hours. She remained under criminal indictment for 22 days. Judge Micko dismissed the charges Friday with prejudice, ensuring they can never be refiled, and noted the government hasn't produced a single piece of evidence against any of the 39 defendants. In North Carolina, former Lieutenant Governor Mark Robinson built his entire political career on lecturing voters about Christian family values. He was later exposed as the author of posts on a pornography site where he called himself a "black NAZI" and advocated for reinstating slavery. Voters rejected him by 15 points in a state Trump won the same night. This week, on a pastor's podcast, he admitted he had lied to the public and would do it again because he was protecting Donald Trump's 2024 campaign. Texas Governor Greg Abbott officially designated the country's premier Muslim civil rights organization a terrorist group, a label the State Department has never applied. He used that Texas-only designation to block every Islamic school from a billion-dollar voucher program designed to fund religious education. The state made more than 2,200 schools eligible for Texas families. Not a single one was Muslim until the families sued and a federal judge forced the door open. Thirty-eight people are still facing federal charges built on flimsy evidence and civil rights statutes turned inside out. Call the U.S. Attorney's office in Minnesota and demand Pam Bondi drop every one of them. We've emailed 6,600+ stories over 415 straight nights. The algorithm doesn't always show them to you. Subscribe now at govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Today on the Website: https://go.govbrief.today/260321-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/lewis-charges [2] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-mar21 [3] https://go.govbrief.today/ice-tsa [4] https://go.govbrief.today/phillips-waffle-house [5] https://go.govbrief.today/mueller-dies [6] https://go.govbrief.today/stitt-trump [7] https://go.govbrief.today/trans-athletes [8] https://go.govbrief.today/robinson-lie [9] https://go.govbrief.today/islamic-vouchers Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos
